page: Lineages and Histories book (HBO Game of Thrones)
html:
Note: This article is about content invented for the television adaptation Game of Thrones developed by HBO. Although this is a licensed derivative work based upon A Song of Ice and Fire by George R. R. Martin, it is not written by him.
The material on this page should not be considered canonical for the novels, and is provided for informational purposes only.
Depiction of the book in Game of Thrones

The Lineages and Histories of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms is a book prop that appears in the HBO television show Game of Thrones, in Season 1. It is based on the book The Lineages and Histories of the Great Houses of the Seven Kingdoms, With Descriptions of Many High Lords and Noble Ladies and Their Children by Maester Malleon, which lists the lineages of the great houses of Westeros.[1] The prop appears in the episodes "Cripples, Bastards, and Broken Things" and "A Golden Crown".

Contents[ ]

In the episodes, some pages of the book were visible, and displayed non-canonical family tree information for several houses.[2][3]

House Baratheon[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

Lord ORYS BARATHEON

  • his wife, Lady ARGELLA DURRANDON
  • their children:
    • AXEL (2-44 AC), m. Lady Alanna Penrose, died in a duel with Steffon Trant
    • SERAC (5-73 AC), m. Monica Velaryon
      • Serac's eldest son, WILLIAM, m. Martyna Mullendore
        • William's sons, WILLIAM, JON, MARTYN, MACE, ORYS, THEODOR, BRYTE, TOMMAX, LORAX, BRUNE, GUDE, and LEX
        • William's daughters, WINEIRI, MIRIAM, and ARGELLA
      • Serac's second son, REGINALD
      • Serac's third son, STEFFON
      • Serac's fourth son, PADRAIC (29-? AC)
      • Serac's youngest son, FLYNN
    • ETHELIDE (7-? AC), eloped with a hedge knight
    • THERESA (9-? AC)
    • Ser MYCHAL (11-41 AC), a member of the Kingsguard of Aenys I Targaryen, under Lord Commander Serwyn Errol

Lord SYMEON BARATHEON

  • his wife, Lady MEERA
    • their son, LYONEL (204-249 AC), m. Rhaelle Targaryen[N 1]
      • Lyonel's son, STEFFON[N 2] (229-278 AC), m. Cassana Estermont
        • Steffon's eldest son, ROBERT, m. Cersei Lannister
          • Robert's eldest son, JOFFREY
          • Robert's second son, TOMMEN
          • Robert's daughter, MYRCELLA
        • Steffon's second son, STANNIS
        • Steffon's youngest son, RENLY

House Royce[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

ROBAR ROYCE, Lord of Runestone

  • his wife, Lady DIANA
  • their sons:
    • MAYNARD (182-220 AC), m. Lady Mavis Belmore
      • Maynard's elder son ANDAR, m. Lady Elena Egen, daughter of Lord Xander Egen and Lady Rusalia
        • Andar's sons, YOHN, MAXIM, and WAYMAR
      • Maynard's younger son PAXTER (206-246 AC), m. Lady Wylla Waynwood
        • Paxter's daughter, EDWINA
      • Maynard's elder daughter JUNIA (209-226 AC), murdered by a jealous suitor, Brammax Egen
      • Maynard's younger daughter SERENA, wed to Ser Stentey Hunter
    • DONAL, m. Priscilla
      • Donal's elder son, DONAL (202-? AC), m. Catherine Hornwood
        • Donal's stillborn son
        • Donal's daughters, MAVIS, PRUEA, PHILLIPPA, DANEALA, JUNIA, and LUNA
      • Donal's younger son, WAYRICK (205-? AC), a member of the Night's Watch
    • sons

House Hunter[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

Ser STENTEY HUNTER

  • his wife, SERENA ROYCE
    • their sons, DAREON, SIMON, STEPHUS, MAYNARD, LIONEL, GILWOOD, and SAMWELL
    • their daughters, DORGAS, SANSA, and MILLICENT

House Targaryen[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

King VISERYS II TARGARYEN (?-172 AC)

  • his wife, VISNEYA[sic] TARGARYEN[N 3]
  • their children:
    • King AEGON IV (135–184 AC), m. Princess NAERYS TARGARYEN
      • Aegon IV's son King DAERON II (159–209 AC), m. Princess Myriah Martell
        • Daeron II's sons, BEALOR, AERYS, RHAEGAL, and MAEKAR
        • Daeron II's daughter AELINOR[N 4]
      • Aegon IV's daughter Princess DAENERYS (182–202 AC), m. Prince Maron Martell
        • Daenerys' stillborn child[N 5]
    • Aegon IV's bastard son by DAENA TARGARYEN:
      • DAEMON BLACKFYRE (170–196 AC), m. Serena
    • Aegon IV's bastard children:
      • AEGOR RIVERS, BRYNDEN RIVERS, SHIERA SEASTAR
    • Queen NAERYS, wed to King AEGON IV TARGARYEN
    • Prince AEMON (140–180 AC)

House Blackfyre[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

King DAEMON I BLACKFYRE (claimant)

  • his wife, SERENA[N 6]
    • their sons:
      • Daemon I's eldest son, AEGON (183–196 AC)
      • Daemon I's second son, AEMON (183–196 AC)
      • Daemon I's third son, DAEMON II (148–? AC)

House Umber[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

Lord DARAGH UMBER

  • his wife, Lady MORAG
    • their son, HOTHOR (162-? AC), m. Lady Amarylis Manderly
      • Hothor's eldest son, HARKON (183-197 AC)
      • Hothor's elder daughter, REGAN (186-256 AC), wed to Tommard Karstark
      • Hothor's second son, MYCAH (190-225 AC), a member of the Night's Watch
      • Hothor's third son, LUCAN (193-196 AC)
      • Hothor's fourth son, GARETH (196-256 AC), m. Lady Hollys Tallhart, daughter of Lord Tyrek Tallhart and Lady Petra
        • Gareth's eldest son, BRANDON (220-? AC), married, with issue
        • Gareth's second son, KEAR (225-275 AC), a member of the Night's Watch
        • Gareth's third son, FINN (227-271 AC), m. Marya Greenleaf, daughter of Mickon Greenleaf, Warden of Last Hearth
          • Finn's daughters, BENYA, TULIP, and CAREN
        • Gareth's elder daughter, EMILIA (228-? AC), wed to Ser Jore Blackbrow
        • Gareth's fourth son, IAIN (230-270 AC), m. Arwen Swann, daughter of Lord Ector Swann and Eleanor
          • Iain's son, IAIN
        • Gareth's younger daughter, MORGANA (232-? AC)
      • Hothor's fifth son, JOSETH (198-238 AC), m. Lady Katrina Cerwyn, daughter of Lord Cleos Umber and Lady Vivian Cerwyn
        • Joseth's sons, MAYNARD and CEDRIC
      • Hothor's younger daughter, CORIN (200-? AC)

House Blackbrow[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

Lord ERICH BLACKBROW

  • his wife, Lady CONN
    • their son, Ser JORE BLACKBROW, m. Emilia Umber
      • Jore's sons, MAREK and HELOS
      • Jore's daughter, EMILIA

House Karstark[ ]

For the family tree, see below.

Lord BERIC KARSTARK

  • his wife, Lady MARA
    • their son, TOMMARD, m. Regan Umber
      • Tommard's sons, BERRICAN, UTHOR, PETYR, JON, and LAURENCE

Notes[ ]

  1. ↑ In the novels, Rhaelle Targaryen was married to Ormund Baratheon, Lord Lyonel Baratheon's heir.
  2. ↑ In the novels, Steffon Baratheon was the son of Ormund Baratheon and Rhaelle Targaryen.
  3. ↑ In the novels, Viserys II Targaryen was married to Larra Rogare, but also had a stillborn sister, Visenya Targaryen.
  4. ↑ In the novels, Aelinor was not a Targaryen, but a Penrose, although this was stated incorrectly in A Clash of Kings.[4]
  5. ↑ In the novels, Daenerys and Maron had multiple living children.
  6. ↑ In the novels, Daemon I Blackfyre was married to Rohanne of Tyrosh.

References[ ]

  1. ↑ A Game of Thrones, Chapter 25, Eddard V.
  2. ↑ Game of Thrones, Season 1, "Cripples, Bastards, and Broken Things".
  3. ↑ Game of Thrones, Season 1, "A Golden Crown".
  4. ↑ A Clash of Kings, Chapter 6, Jon I.
